name: Prik Kaeng Kiao Wan (Green Curry-sweet/hot)
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Prik ki nu (green birdseye chilis)
recipeInstructions:
This is a paste for a green curry, and the 'wan' indicates that it should be slightly sweet as well as hot. If you can't get Prik ki nu, use half a pound of habanero chilis or one pound of jalapeno chilis. Deseed them before use. Note that using substitutes will affect paste volume and subsequent recipe measurements.
If unavailable:
- Use ginger instead of kha
- Use lime zest instead of bai makroot
- Use coriander leaves instead of coriander root
1. Coarsely chop the chilis.
2. Toast dry seeds in a heavy iron skillet or wok, then grind coarsely.
3. Add all ingredients to a food processor and process into a smooth paste.
4. Store in tightly stoppered jars in the fridge for at least a week for flavors to combine before use.
Note: If using dried red chilis (sold in Thailand/Europe/ America), soak in tepid water for 30 minutes before cooking. Toast until almost burnt, treating with caution due to toxic gas release. Keep well-ventilated and have a damp cloth ready to cover mouth/nose in emergencies. Thai dishes use 'kaeng' paste, not just 'curry'. Use the pastes for soups, stews, or curries by adjusting protein and vegetable ingredients. Scale up the recipe as needed, yielding about 3 months of preserved paste in the fridge.
